{
    "success": true,
    "data": [
        {
            "_id": "69a1f0acdfc7906b182c3468",
            "title": "12 Rules for Life",
            "authorId": {
                "_id": "69a1ef85dfc7906b182c3464",
                "name": "Jordan Peterson"
            },
            "narratorId": {
                "_id": "69a1eedbdfc7906b182c3461",
                "name": "Ruby Parikh"
            },
            "description": "12 Rules for Life: An Antidote to Chaos is a philosophical self-help guide that argues the best way to fix the world is to first fix yourself. Using a blend of ancient mythology, evolutionary biology, and clinical psychology, Peterson presents a framework for finding meaning in a world he views as inherently tragic and chaotic.\n\nThe book is built on the idea that humans thrive when they balance Order (structure and safety) with Chaos (potential and the unknown). Rather than pursuing happiness-which he considers fleeting-Peterson encourages readers to pursue responsibility, arguing that taking ownership of one’s life provides the strength to endure suffering.",
            "categoryId": {
                "_id": "699fd464645d44c937ddc85e",
                "name": "Psychology"
            },
            "audioUrl": "https://res.cloudinary.com/dvrlt0x85/video/upload/v1772220586/audiobooks/doxlon7lm9etn487ngsr.mp3",
            "audioPublicId": "audiobooks/doxlon7lm9etn487ngsr",
            "coverImageUrl": "https://res.cloudinary.com/dvrlt0x85/image/upload/v1772220589/audiobooks/covers/hmy1jk2dqmslqxdtu1rv.png",
            "coverImagePublicId": "audiobooks/covers/hmy1jk2dqmslqxdtu1rv",
            "durationInSeconds": 1887.164082,
            "averageRating": 0,
            "reviewsCount": 0,
            "likesCount": 0,

            "createdAt": "2026-02-27T19:29:48.855Z",
            "updatedAt": "2026-02-27T19:29:48.855Z",
            "__v": 0
        },
        {
            "averageRating": 0,
            "reviewsCount": 0,
            "_id": "699fd6a4645d44c937ddc86b",
            "title": "Brain Rules",
            "description": "Brain Rules (2008) by Dr. John Medina is a science-backed guide that explains how the human brain functions and how to apply this knowledge to improve work, school, and home life. A molecular biologist, Medina breaks down complex neuroscience into 12 \"rules\" that describe our brain's core abilities and limitations.\nThe book's core premise is that modern environments—like high-stress cubicles and sedentary classrooms—are often designed in direct opposition to how our brains evolved to function. By understanding evolutionary biology, we can use our natural impulses to enhance learning, productivity, and health.",
            "categoryId": {
                "_id": "699fd464645d44c937ddc85e",
                "name": "Psychology"
            },
            "audioUrl": "https://res.cloudinary.com/dvrlt0x85/video/upload/v1772082847/audiobooks/iml7jx3s3kgl0pg90fqj.mp3",
            "audioPublicId": "audiobooks/iml7jx3s3kgl0pg90fqj",
            "coverImageUrl": "https://res.cloudinary.com/dvrlt0x85/image/upload/v1772082851/audiobooks/covers/gyzt1hmdtwgh0kqpsdnj.png",
            "coverImagePublicId": "audiobooks/covers/gyzt1hmdtwgh0kqpsdnj",
            "durationInSeconds": 1698.586122,
            "likesCount": 2,
            "commentsCount": 0,
            "createdAt": "2026-02-26T05:14:12.705Z",
            "updatedAt": "2026-02-27T10:44:30.090Z",
            "__v": 0,
            "authorId": {
                "_id": "69a1df10f3c997c663628851",
                "name": "Dr. John Medina"
            },
            "narratorId": {
                "_id": "69a1eedbdfc7906b182c3461",
                "name": "Ruby Parikh"
            }
        },
        {
            "averageRating": 0,
            "reviewsCount": 0,
            "_id": "699fd56c645d44c937ddc868",
            "title": "Better Than Before",
            "description": "Better Than Before (2015) by Gretchen Rubin is a self-help guide that argues the secret to changing habits is self-knowledge. It rejects \"one-size-fits-all\" solutions, instead offering a framework called the Four Tendencies—Upholder, Questioner, Obliger, and Rebel—to help you identify how you personally respond to expectations.\nThe book identifies 21 strategies to make or break habits, such as monitoring (tracking behavior), scheduling (setting specific times), and accountability (using external pressure). By understanding your own personality type, you can choose the specific tools that will actually work for your life.",
            "categoryId": {
                "_id": "699fd458645d44c937ddc85b",
                "name": "Self-Help"
            },
            "audioUrl": "https://res.cloudinary.com/dvrlt0x85/video/upload/v1772082538/audiobooks/w1se17e9tlzfuxnpqb91.mp3",
            "audioPublicId": "audiobooks/w1se17e9tlzfuxnpqb91",
            "coverImageUrl": "https://res.cloudinary.com/dvrlt0x85/image/upload/v1772082541/audiobooks/covers/fdccatxtvt2jfmggfqvs.png",
            "coverImagePublicId": "audiobooks/covers/fdccatxtvt2jfmggfqvs",
            "durationInSeconds": 1442.142041,

        {
            "_id": "69a1f2e5dfc7906b182c3471",
            "title": "Good to Great",
            "authorId": {
                "_id": "69a1f280dfc7906b182c346e",
                "name": "Jim Collins"
            },

                "_id": "69a1eedbdfc7906b182c3461",
                "name": "Ruby Parikh"
            },
            "description": "Good to Great is the result of a five-year research study comparing companies that made the leap to long-term greatness against a control group of similar companies that failed to do so. formdata.value: Good to Great is the result of a five-year research study comparing companies that made the leap to long-term greatness against a control group of similar companies that failed to do so. Collins identifies specific concepts—such as Level 5 Leadership, the Hedgehog Concept, and the Flywheel effect—that allow a company to transition from mediocrity to sustained excellence.

12 Rules for Life: An Antidote to Chaos is a philosophical self-help guide that argues the best way to fix the world is to first fix yourself. Using a blend of ancient mythology, evolutionary biology, and clinical psychology, Peterson presents a framework for finding meaning in a world he views as inherently tragic and chaotic.

The book is built on the idea that humans thrive when they balance Order (structure and safety) with Chaos (potential and the unknown). Rather than pursuing happiness-which he considers fleeting-Peterson encourages readers to pursue responsibility, arguing that taking ownership of one’s life provides the strength to endure suffering.

response.body:

Using a blend of ancient mythology, evolutionary biology, and clinical psychology, Peterson presents a framework for finding meaning in a world he views as inherently tragic and chaotic.\n\nThe book is built on the idea that humans thrive when they balance Order (structure and safety) with Chaos (potential and the unknown).         "_id": "69a1f2e5dfc7906b182c3471",

        "authorId": {
            "_id": "69a1f280dfc7906b182c346e",
            "name": "Jim Collins",
            "biography": "Jim Collins is an American researcher, author, and speaker who focuses on business management and corporate sustainability. A former faculty member at the Stanford Graduate School of Business, he is widely regarded as a 'student of companies,' using massive data sets to understand why some businesses thrive while others fail."
        },
        "narratorId": {
            "_id": "69a1eedbdfc7906b182c3461",

                "description": "Brain Rules (2008) by Dr. John Medina is a science-backed guide that explains how the human brain functions and how to apply this knowledge to improve work, school, and home life. A molecular biologist, Medina breaks down complex neuroscience into 12 \"rules\" that describe our brain's core abilities and limitations.\nThe book's core premise is that modern environments—like high-stress cubicles and sedentary classrooms—are often designed in direct opposition to how our brains evolved to function. By understanding evolutionary biology, we can use our natural impulses to enhance learning, productivity, and health.",
                "categoryId": {
                    "_id": "699fd464645d44c937ddc85e",
                    "name": "Psychology"
                },
                "audioUrl": "https://res.cloudinary.com/dvrlt0x85/video/upload/v1772082847/audiobooks/iml7jx3s3kgl0pg90fqj.mp3",

            {
                "averageRating": 0,

                "_id": "699fd56c645d44c937ddc868",
                "title": "Better Than Before",
                "description": "Better Than Before (2015) by Gretchen Rubin is a self-help guide that argues the secret to changing habits is self-knowledge. It rejects \"one-size-fits-all\" solutions, instead offering a framework called the Four Tendencies—Upholder, Questioner, Obliger, and Rebel—to help you identify how you personally respond to expectations.\nThe book identifies 21 strategies to make or break habits, such as monitoring (tracking behavior), scheduling (setting specific times), and accountability (using external pressure). By understanding your own personality type, you can choose the specific tools that will actually work for your life.",
                "categoryId": {
                    "_id": "699fd458645d44c937ddc85b",
                    "name": "Self-Help"
                },
                "audioUrl": "https://res.cloudinary.com/dvrlt0x85/video/upload/v1772082538/audiobooks/w1se17e9tlzfuxnpqb91.mp3",

            {
                "_id": "69a1f0acdfc7906b182c3468",
                "title": "12 Rules for Life",
                "authorId": {
                    "_id": "69a1ef85dfc7906b182c3464",
                    "name": "Jordan Peterson"
                },
                "narratorId": "69a1eedbdfc7906b182c3461",
                "description": "12 Rules for Life: An Antidote to Chaos is a philosophical self-help guide that argues the best way to fix the world is to first fix yourself. Using a blend of ancient mythology, evolutionary biology, and clinical psychology, Peterson presents a framework for finding meaning in a world he views as inherently tragic and chaotic.\n\nThe book is built on the idea that humans thrive when they balance Order (structure and safety) with Chaos (potential and the unknown). Rather than pursuing happiness-which he considers fleeting-Peterson encourages readers to pursue responsibility, arguing that taking ownership of one’s life provides the strength to endure suffering.",
                "categoryId": {
                    "_id": "699fd464645d44c937ddc85e",
                    "name": "Psychology"
                },
                "audioUrl": "https://res.cloudinary.com/dvrlt0x85/video/upload/v1772220586/audiobooks/doxlon7lm9etn487ngsr.mp3",

            {
                "_id": "69a1f2e5dfc7906b182c3471",
                "title": "Good to Great",
                "authorId": {
                    "_id": "69a1f280dfc7906b182c346e",
                    "name": "Jim Collins"
                },
                "narratorId": "69a1eedbdfc7906b182c3461",
                "description": "Good to Great is the result of a five-year research study comparing companies that made the leap to long-term greatness against a control group of similar companies that failed to do so. Collins identifies specific concepts—such as Level 5 Leadership, the Hedgehog Concept, and the Flywheel effect—that allow a company to transition from mediocrity to sustained excellence.",

{
    "success": true,
    "count": 4,
    "data": [
        {
            "_id": "69a1df10f3c997c663628851",
            "name": "Dr. John Medina",
            "createdAt": "2026-02-27T18:14:40.996Z",
            "updatedAt": "2026-02-27T19:47:37.164Z",
            "__v": 0,
            "biography": "Dr. John Medina is a developmental molecular biologist and a leading expert on how the brain reacts to and organizes information. He serves as an affiliate professor of Bioengineering at the University of Washington School of Medicine and directed the Brain Center for Applied Learning Research at Seattle Pacific University. A gifted communicator, he is famous for bridging the gap between complex neuroscience and everyday life. His work focuses on brain development, the genetics of psychiatric disorders, and creating 'brain-friendly' environments for schools and offices."
        },
        {
            "_id": "69a1df0df3c997c66362884e",
            "name": "Gretchen Rubin",
            "createdAt": "2026-02-27T18:14:37.219Z",
            "updatedAt": "2026-02-27T19:49:27.906Z",
            "__v": 0,
            "biography": "Gretchen Rubin is an American author and speaker who graduated from Yale Law School and began her career clerking for Supreme Court Justice Sandra Day O’Connor. She realized her true passion was writing and has since become one of the most influential observers of human nature and happiness. She is the founder of 'The Happiness Project' ecosystem and co-hosts the popular podcast Happier with Gretchen Rubin. Her work is known for distilling complex research from science and philosophy into practical, actionable advice for everyday life."
        },
        {
            "_id": "69a1f280dfc7906b182c346e",
            "name": "Jim Collins",
            "biography": "Jim Collins is an American researcher, author, and speaker who focuses on business management and corporate sustainability. A former faculty member at the Stanford Graduate School of Business, he is widely regarded as a 'student of companies,' using massive data sets to understand why some businesses thrive while others fail.",
            "createdAt": "2026-02-27T19:37:36.070Z",
            "updatedAt": "2026-02-27T19:37:36.070Z",
            "__v": 0
        },
        {
            "_id": "69a1ef85dfc7906b182c3464",
            "name": "Jordan Peterson",
            "biography": "Jordan Peterson is a Canadian clinical psychologist and professor who became a global phenomenon for his views on personal responsibility and free speech. He rose to fame through his academic lectures on YouTube, blending mythology, psychology, and evolutionary biology to help people find meaning. His 2018 bestseller, 12 Rules for Life, advocates for self-discipline and 'cleaning your room' as a foundation for a stable society. While a polarizing figure in cultural politics, he has influenced millions with his focus on the balance between order and chaos.",
            "createdAt": "2026-02-27T19:24:53.290Z",
            "updatedAt": "2026-02-27T19:24:53.290Z",
            "__v": 0
        }
    ]
}
